channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A Modern Mapping Mashup: PamPam's Upgrade
item.description: Discover the latest upgrade to PamPam, an AI map app that seamlessly blends classic and modern tech concepts.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: The next wave of AI is here: Autonomous AI agents are amazing—and scary
item.description: The relentless hype around AI makes it difficult to separate the signal from the noise. So it’s understandable if you’ve tuned out recent talk about autonomous AI agents. A word of advice: Don’t. The significance of agentic AI may actually exceed the hype.
item.dc:creator: Tom Barnett, Fast Company

item.title: The end of an era? TSMC, Broadcom could tear apart Intel's legendary business after 57 years by separating its foundry and chip design
item.description: Broadcom and TSMC reportedly looking to chop Intel in half, with separate interests in its foundry and chip design.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Toyota's Woven City: A Futuristic Hub for Robotics and AI Innovation
item.description: Toyota's ambitious project, Woven City, is a testbed for everyday living with robotics, AI, and autonomous zero-emissions transportation near Mount Fuji.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How to set your preferred web browser on your desktop and mobile devices
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy takes you through the steps to easily set your preferred web browser whether you're using a Mac, Windows PC, iPhone or Android.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Handmaid's Tale season 6: everything we know so far about the hit Hulu show’s return
item.description: The Handmaid's Tale season 6 will be available to stream on Hulu in April 2025 – here's everything we know about the dystopian drama's return, while we wait.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The government or 4chan? The White House’s social media account is sparking outrage
item.description: The official White House social media account is under fire for posts that resemble something typically found on the internet forum 4chan. A post shared on February 14, styled like a Valentine’s card, read, “Roses are red, violets are blue, come here illegally and we’ll deport you.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: How Wikipedia became a political lightning rod
item.description: Wikipedia has faced political threats for years, but this time, it may be at a breaking point. Republicans have ramped up attacks against Wikipedia as yet another “woke” institution.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: A Brazilian Supreme Court justice has ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Savarese, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Majority of Americans dislike Musk, Zuckerberg: Poll
item.description: A Pew Research Center poll shows 54% of Americans have an "unfavorable" view of Musk, while 67% have an "unfavorable" view of Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rivian records first gross profit as EV policy risks loom
item.description: The California-based company's fourth quarter result easily topped the $32 million expected by Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves could be 2025's best fighting game, and you can try it right now
item.description: The Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves Open Beta is live now, here's everything you need to start playing.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: GenAI is my copilot? Developers play whack-a-hallucination
item.description: Developers are quicker than most to embrace generative AI tools as part of their workflow. That can mean enhanced productivity and easing cognitive burdens, but it also means learning new technologies while they’re still being built.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Deno update brings OpenTelemetry integration
item.description: Deno Land has released Deno 2.2, a new version of the Deno runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript that features built-in OpenTelemetry for monitoring logs, metrics, and traces. The new Deno release also features Linter updates that include a new plugin system and new rules for React and Preact users.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apollo GraphQL ships connectors for REST APIs
item.description: Apollo GraphQL, provider of the GraphOS API orchestration platform, has introduced Apollo Connectors for REST APIs, a way to integrate REST APIs into a composable GraphQL architecture without writing boilerplate code. The company also announced GraphOS platform enhancements.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk calls former International Space Station commander 'idiot' in exchange over astronauts
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk called a former commander of the International Space Station an “idiot” on Thursday after he pushed back on Musk’s claim that the Biden administration “left” two astronauts in space “for political reasons.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Chaos ahead: emerging technologies will test data privacy, says Britain’s ICO
item.description: Emerging technologies such as connected transport, quantum sensing, healthcare diagnostics, and synthetic AI-driven content are creating huge gaps in data protection and privacy that could quickly outpace the ability of regulators and enterprises to contain them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Versa Networks launches sovereign SASE, challenging cloud-only security model
item.description: SASE since its inception has typically been deployed in a software-as-a-service (SaaS) model, delivering network security services from the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition
item.description: Learn how OSINT is shaping the future of intelligence and security. The post OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Bannon: ‘Oligarchs’ will abandon Republicans
item.description: Steve Bannon argued that the “oligarchs” close to President Trump will abandon Republicans the same way they did to Democrats. Bannon was briefly interviewed by CNN’s Donie O’Sullivan on Wednesday, when he continued his case against tech billionaires Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati launches AI startup, recruits top talent from rivals
item.description: Former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati has launched an AI startup, Thinking Machines Lab, escalating the fight for top talent and reshaping competition in the fast-evolving sector.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft updates VS Code C# Dev Kit extension
item.description: Microsoft announced updates to its C# Dev Kit for Visual Studio Code extension, including enhancements to Razor/Blazor Hot Reload and for debugging and testing Blazor web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft unveils company's first quantum computing chip
item.description: Microsoft on Wednesday revealed its first quantum computing chip, Majorana 1, stating it will speed up the development of quantum computers able to solve "meaningful, industrial-scale problems in years, not decades.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Satellite Connectivity on iPhones to Improve with New Globalstar Constellation - Tech Update
item.description: Apple and satellite partner Globalstar have launched a new satellite constellation, promising improved connectivity for iPhone users worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5
